api_requirements: FEMA Flood Map Service Center API
data_flow: Project coordinates → Multi-API hazard data calls → Data aggregation & risk scoring → Climate scenario modeling → Report generation → USGBC submission
authentication: API keys for NOAA/FEMA (free registration), OAuth for commercial climate data providers, secure handling of project location data
notes: High feasibility due to well-documented government APIs. NOAA and FEMA provide robust free APIs. Challenge: Integrating multiple hazard types into unified risk assessment. Solution: Create middleware API aggregator.

data_flow: Project address → Geocoding → Census tract identification → Multi-source demographic pull → Equity scoring → Community context analysis → Report generation
authentication: Census API key (free), EPA EJScreen (public), HUD API key, local portal credentials vary
notes: Excellent API availability. Census Bureau and EPA provide comprehensive free APIs. EJScreen offers pre-calculated environmental justice metrics. Challenge: Normalizing data across different geographic scales.

api_requirements: EC3 API (Embodied Carbon in Construction Calculator)
software_integration: Energy modeling software (EnergyPlus, IES VE, TRACE 700)
data_flow: Energy model outputs + Material quantities → EC3/Tally API calls → Grid emission factors → 25-year projection modeling → Decarbonization pathway analysis → Report generation
authentication: EPA eGRID (public), EC3 (free registration), Tally (license key), One Click LCA (API token)
notes: Strong integration potential. EC3 and Tally have growing API capabilities. Energy modeling software increasingly offers APIs. Challenge: Real-time sync between energy models and carbon calculations.

api_requirements: Cloud storage APIs (AWS S3, Azure Blob)
data_flow: Project specifications + LEED credit strategies → Template population → Guideline document generation → Review workflow → Distribution to tenants
authentication: USGBC Arc platform OAuth, cloud storage service credentials
integration_frequency: Batch - per project, updated as design evolves
notes: Primarily document generation with moderate API needs. Challenge: Keeping guidelines synchronized with evolving design. Solution: Automated document versioning with change tracking.

data_flow: Team roster + Project goals → Charrette planning → Meeting coordination → Goal tracking → Documentation → OPR integration
authentication: OAuth 2.0 for project management platforms, API keys for collaboration tools
integration_frequency: Real-time during design phases, batch for reporting
notes: Lower integration score due to human-centric process. APIs support coordination but core activity is collaborative design. Challenge: Capturing qualitative charrette outcomes. Solution: Structured data collection templates.

data_flow: Base building specs + LEED requirements → Lease clause generation → Green Lease Leaders alignment check → Legal review → Final lease document
authentication: Green Lease Leaders (registration required), document platform credentials
notes: Moderate feasibility. Green Lease Leaders program provides guidance but limited API access. Challenge: Legal review integration. Solution: Structured clause libraries with automated compliance checking.

api_requirements: FEMA Flood Map Service Center API
data_flow: Site boundaries → Multi-layer GIS analysis → Sensitivity scoring → Compliance determination → Map generation → Documentation
authentication: Most government APIs are public or free registration. Some USGS services require API keys.
notes: Excellent integration potential. Government environmental APIs are mature and well-documented. Automated GIS analysis can quickly identify sensitive land constraints.

api_requirements: National Register of Historic Places API
data_flow: Site location → Brownfield/historic status check → Workforce data analysis → Housing market analysis → Pathway recommendation → Points calculation
notes: Strong API ecosystem for site classification. Brownfields and historic registers have searchable databases. Challenge: Local workforce data may require manual integration.

data_flow: Site address → Density calculations (Census/assessor) → Transit trip analysis (GTFS) → Walk Score API call → Combined scoring → Points determination
authentication: Walk Score (API key), Google Maps (paid API key), GTFS (public), Census (free key)
notes: High feasibility with mature location APIs. Walk Score provides single-call walkability metrics. GTFS enables custom transit accessibility calculations. Challenge: GTFS feed availability varies by region.

data_flow: Project type + Occupancy → Trip generation lookup → VMT estimation → Carbon emission calculation → Parking ratio analysis → Facility sizing → TDM plan
integration_frequency: Batch - per project, updated with design changes
notes: Good integration potential. ITE provides standardized trip generation data. Challenge: Regional VMT data availability varies. Solution: Use national defaults with local calibration options.

data_flow: Parking count + Building type → EVSE quantity calculation → Electrical load analysis → Accessible space requirements → Equipment specification → Compliance verification
notes: Straightforward calculations with good API support. ENERGY STAR EVSE registry helps identify qualified equipment. Challenge: Electrical load integration with building systems.

data_flow: Site survey data + Satellite imagery → Vegetation classification → Invasive species check → Erosion control plan generation → Exclusion zone mapping
authentication: USDA (public), EPA (varies), satellite imagery (API keys required)
notes: Good integration with vegetation and soil databases. Satellite imagery APIs enable automated vegetation mapping. Challenge: Accurate invasive species identification may require expert review.

data_flow: Site plan + Vegetation survey → Habitat area calculations → Bird collision risk analysis → Restoration specifications → Compliance verification
authentication: Varies by database, many ecological resources are public
notes: Moderate integration potential. Bird collision analysis tools exist but may require manual input. Challenge: Local ecological data may not be API-accessible.

data_flow: Site plan + Building footprint → Outdoor space area calculation → Seating requirements → Accessibility path check → Compliance verification
notes: Primarily calculation-based with BIM integration for area measurements. Lower score due to limited specialized APIs for outdoor accessibility.

data_flow: Site area + Impervious coverage → Rainfall data (NOAA) → Soil infiltration rates → Runoff calculations → LID/GI sizing → Compliance verification
integration_frequency: Batch - per design iteration, real-time for monitoring
notes: Excellent integration. NOAA provides comprehensive rainfall data APIs. Stormwater modeling software increasingly offers APIs. Challenge: Local stormwater requirements vary significantly.

data_flow: IPp1 priority hazards → Strategy matching → Design specifications → Compliance verification → Documentation
notes: Good integration leveraging IPp1 data. Strategy databases can be pre-populated with hazard-response mappings. Challenge: Local code variations require customization.

api_requirements: American Forests Tree Equity Score API
data_flow: Site plan + Material specs → SRI lookups → Area calculations → Tree Equity Score lookup → Weighted compliance calculation → Points determination
authentication: Tree Equity Score (public), CRRC (public), material databases
notes: Strong integration potential. Tree Equity Score API provides location-based canopy targets. Material databases offer SRI values. Challenge: Real-time tree canopy analysis may require satellite imagery processing.

data_flow: Luminaire schedule → BUG rating lookup → Lighting zone determination → Compliance checking → Non-compliant fixture identification → Alternatives recommendation
notes: Excellent integration. BUG ratings are standardized and many manufacturers provide API access to product data. Automated compliance checking is straightforward.

data_flow: Water systems inventory → Meter specification → Installation → Data collection → Arc platform integration → 5-year reporting commitment
authentication: Arc platform OAuth, meter manufacturer APIs, BMS credentials
integration_frequency: Real-time for metering, batch for reporting
notes: Strong integration potential. USGBC Arc platform has documented API. Smart water meters increasingly offer API access. Challenge: Protocol standardization across meter manufacturers.

data_flow: Fixture schedule + Appliance specs → Flow rate verification → Water use calculations → ENERGY STAR check → Irrigation analysis → Compliance report
notes: Excellent integration. ENERGY STAR and WaterSense provide robust APIs. Fixture compliance checking is straightforward. Challenge: Irrigation calculations require local climate data integration.

data_flow: Building water systems → Submeter placement planning → Sensor specification → Installation → Data integration → Leak detection analytics → Action plan generation
authentication: Sensor manufacturer APIs, BMS credentials, analytics platform tokens
integration_frequency: Real-time for monitoring, batch for reporting
notes: Good integration with IoT sensor ecosystems. Leak detection systems increasingly API-enabled. Challenge: Integration with existing BMS may require custom middleware.

data_flow: All water uses + Alternative sources → Water balance modeling → Reduction percentage calculation → Alternative source integration → Optimization → Points determination
authentication: NOAA (public), local regulation databases, modeling software licenses
notes: Strong integration potential. Water modeling tools offer APIs. Alternative source availability can be automated with rainfall and regulatory data. Challenge: Local water reuse regulations vary significantly.

credit_name: Operational Carbon Projection and Decarbonization Plan
data_flow: Energy model outputs → Grid emission factors → 25-year projection → Decarbonization pathway analysis → Electrification planning → Documentation
authentication: Energy modeling APIs (license-based), EPA (public), utility APIs (varies)
notes: Strong integration. Energy modeling software increasingly offers APIs. Grid emission factors available through EPA. Challenge: Long-term grid decarbonization projections require assumptions.

data_flow: Building specifications → Baseline model generation → Proposed model → Performance comparison → Prescriptive checklist → Compliance verification
notes: Good integration. Energy modeling APIs enable automated compliance checking. Challenge: ASHRAE 90.1 baseline generation is complex and may require expert oversight.

data_flow: Project schedule + Systems inventory → Cx plan generation → Milestone tracking → Documentation management → OPR/BOD development
authentication: Project management OAuth, document platform credentials
integration_frequency: Real-time for tracking, batch for documentation
notes: Moderate integration. Process management can be automated but core commissioning is human-driven. Challenge: Capturing Cx Authority expertise and decisions.

data_flow: Energy systems → Metering plan → Installation → Data collection → Arc platform integration → 5-year reporting → Performance tracking
authentication: Arc OAuth, utility Green Button, BMS credentials, meter APIs
integration_frequency: Real-time for metering, batch for Arc reporting
notes: Strong integration. Arc platform API enables automated reporting. Green Button standard facilitates utility data access. Challenge: BMS protocol standardization.

data_flow: HVAC equipment schedule → Refrigerant type lookup → GWP checking → Phase-out schedule verification → Compliance report → Alternative recommendations
notes: Excellent integration. Refrigerant databases are well-documented. Equipment manufacturer APIs increasingly available. Automated compliance checking is straightforward.

data_flow: HVAC equipment + Heating loads → COP calculations → Weighted average analysis → Electrification pathway → Equipment specifications → Points determination
notes: Strong integration. Equipment performance data available through manufacturer APIs. COP calculations can be automated. Challenge: Climate-specific performance variations.

data_flow: Envelope specs + Ventilation design → Air leakage analysis → ERV sizing → Thermal bridge assessment → Peak load modeling → Points calculation
notes: Good integration with energy modeling tools. Thermal bridge detection can be automated with BIM integration. Challenge: Detailed envelope analysis may require specialized software.

data_flow: Energy model + Baseline → Performance comparison → Percent improvement calculation → Optimization analysis → Points determination → Documentation
notes: Strong integration. Energy modeling APIs enable automated performance comparisons. Optimization tools can suggest improvements. Challenge: Baseline model generation complexity.

data_flow: Building energy use + Roof area → Solar potential (PVWatts) → Production estimation → Percent offset calculation → REC procurement → Documentation
authentication: NREL (free API key), Green-e (registration), solar software (licensed)
notes: Strong integration. NREL PVWatts provides excellent solar production API. Green-e enables REC verification. Challenge: Off-site renewable verification requires third-party integration.

data_flow: BMS data streams → Analytics platform → Fault detection → Performance tracking → Optimization recommendations → Documentation
authentication: BMS credentials, analytics platform tokens, MBCx software licenses
integration_frequency: Real-time for MBCx, batch for traditional enhanced Cx
notes: Moderate-high integration. MBCx platforms offer strong API integration. Traditional enhanced Cx is more document-driven. Challenge: BMS data standardization across manufacturers.

data_flow: Building loads + DR programs → Capability assessment → Storage sizing → Grid integration design → Utility program enrollment → Documentation
authentication: Utility APIs (varies), OpenADR (standard), storage system APIs
integration_frequency: Real-time for DR events, batch for design
notes: Good integration potential. OpenADR provides standard DR communication protocol. Challenge: Utility program APIs vary significantly by region.

data_flow: HVAC equipment → Refrigerant charge + GWP → Total impact calculation → Threshold comparison → Low-GWP alternatives → Leak detection specification → Points
notes: Excellent integration. Refrigerant GWP data is standardized. Calculations are straightforward. Leak detection systems increasingly API-enabled.

data_flow: Building type + Occupancy → Waste stream projections → Storage sizing → Service availability check → Operations plan → Documentation
notes: Moderate integration. Waste generation rates are standardized. Local service availability APIs are limited. Challenge: Municipal service integration varies widely.

data_flow: BIM model → Quantity take-off → Material mapping → EC3/Tally API calls → GWP calculations → Hot spot identification → Reduction strategies
authentication: EC3 (free), Tally (license), One Click LCA (API token), EPD databases
notes: Strong integration. EC3 and Tally offer robust APIs for embodied carbon. BIM integration enables automated quantity extraction. Challenge: Material mapping accuracy requires validation.

data_flow: Existing building survey → Area calculations → Salvage inventory → Valuation → Reuse percentage → Compliance documentation
notes: Moderate integration. Existing building data can be extracted from BIM. Salvage market APIs are limited. Challenge: Salvage material valuation requires market data that may not be API-accessible.

api_requirements: WBLCA tool APIs (Tally, One Click LCA)
data_flow: Material quantities + EPDs → WBLCA analysis → Baseline comparison → Reduction percentage → Construction emissions tracking → Points determination
authentication: WBLCA licenses, EC3 (free), EPD registry APIs
integration_frequency: Batch - per design iteration, real-time for construction tracking
notes: Strong integration. WBLCA tools offer comprehensive APIs. EPD registries increasingly accessible. Challenge: Baseline project selection and comparison methodology.

data_flow: Product specifications → Certification lookup → Compliance verification → Category percentage calculation → Points determination → Documentation
notes: Strong integration. GREENGUARD and FloorScore offer searchable databases. HPD repository provides health declaration data. Challenge: Product schedule integration with certification databases.

data_flow: Product specifications → EPD/HPD lookup → Certification validation → Manufacturer count tracking → Points calculation → Documentation
authentication: EPD registries (varies), HPD Collaborative, certification bodies
notes: Good integration. EPD and HPD repositories offer APIs. Certification bodies increasingly provide digital verification. Challenge: Multiple registry integration requires normalization.

data_flow: Waste manifests → Digital tracking → Material stream categorization → Diversion rate calculation → Reporting → Compliance documentation
integration_frequency: Real-time during construction, batch for reporting
notes: Good integration. Digital waste tracking platforms increasingly offer APIs. Challenge: Hauler system integration varies by provider and region.

data_flow: Construction schedule + Weather data → Heat index monitoring → IAQ plan generation → Safety protocol development → Documentation
integration_frequency: Real-time for monitoring, batch for planning
notes: Moderate integration. Weather and safety data available via APIs. Challenge: Construction IAQ monitoring equipment integration may require custom setup.

data_flow: Space types + Occupancy → Ventilation calculations → Outdoor air quality check → Filtration verification → Entryway design → Compliance report
notes: Strong integration. EPA AQI API provides outdoor air quality data. ASHRAE 62.1 calculations can be automated. Challenge: Local air quality may require additional data sources.

data_flow: Building entries + Air intakes → Zone mapping → Signage location planning → Policy document generation → Compliance verification
notes: High integration potential. Mapping APIs enable automated zone calculations. Signage and policy generation can be templated. Straightforward implementation.

data_flow: Baseline ventilation → Enhanced calculations → IAQ design verification → Compliance checking → Points determination
notes: Strong integration. Ventilation calculations can be automated with HVAC design APIs. Enhanced IAQ limits are standardized. Challenge: IAQ modeling may require specialized software.

data_flow: Building geometry + Site context → Daylight analysis → View quality assessment → Acoustic mapping → Thermal comfort analysis → Points aggregation
notes: Good integration. Daylight and acoustic simulation tools offer APIs. BIM integration enables geometry extraction. Challenge: Multiple simulation types require coordination.

data_flow: Design specifications → Accessibility checklist → Gap analysis → Enhanced feature tracking → Compliance documentation
notes: Moderate integration. ADA standards are publicly available. Challenge: Inclusive design goes beyond code minimum and requires expert judgment.

data_flow: IPp1 hazards + Building systems → Resilience strategy verification → Operable window analysis → Thermal safety modeling → Compliance checking
authentication: Internal project data, HVAC software licenses
notes: Good integration leveraging IPp1 data. HVAC and thermal modeling tools provide APIs. Challenge: Resilience strategies require hazard-specific analysis.

data_flow: Building area + Space types → Testing protocol → Sensor specification → Installation → Data collection → Results analysis → Compliance verification
authentication: Sensor APIs, laboratory systems, monitoring platforms
integration_frequency: Real-time for monitoring, batch for testing
notes: Good integration. IAQ sensors increasingly offer API access. Testing protocols can be automated. Challenge: Sensor calibration and data quality assurance.

data_flow: Project location + Type + Pursued credits → Regional priority lookup → Exemplary performance analysis → Pilot credit eligibility → Innovation opportunities → Points optimization
integration_frequency: Batch - per project, updated as credits are pursued
notes: Moderate integration. USGBC provides credit databases. Challenge: Regional priority credits vary and may not be fully API-accessible. Innovation requires human creativity.

data_flow: Team roster → Credential lookup → Verification → Status report → Documentation
integration_frequency: Batch - per team assembly, real-time for verification
notes: Excellent integration. GBCI provides credential verification API. Simple binary check. Challenge: GBCI API access may require partnership agreement.
